---
title: Boards
description: Kanban-style views of a workflow — columns, swimlanes, WIP limits, card templates.
---

Boards visualize a workflow as columns you can drag issues between. Each column is backed by one or more workflow states. Boards don't *own* data — they're pure view config — but they shape how your team sees and moves work.

## File location

`.kiket/boards/*.yaml`. One file per board. A project can have several (e.g. *Engineering sprint*, *Compliance tracker*, *Support triage*).

## Minimum board

```yaml
key: sprint
name: Sprint
workflow: engineering

columns:
  - key: todo
    name: To do
    states: [backlog, ready]
  - key: doing
    name: In progress
    states: [in_progress]
    wip_limit: 3
  - key: done
    name: Done
    states: [done]
```

Every column maps to *one or more* workflow states. Moving an issue between columns fires a transition; if multiple transitions could apply, the board picks the first declared.

## Swimlanes

Group rows by a field — assignee, priority, label, issue type:

```yaml
swimlanes:
  by: assigneeId
  labels:
    strategy: name-then-unassigned
```

Swimlanes are purely visual — they don't affect workflow logic.

## WIP limits

Per-column ceiling. Setting a column's `wip_limit` makes the board surface a warning (and optionally block) when the limit is exceeded. Useful for continuous-flow teams.

```yaml
columns:
  - key: doing
    name: In progress
    states: [in_progress]
    wip_limit: 3
    wip_policy: block   # or 'warn' (default)
```

## Card templates

Control what each card shows. Pick from a field list, or write a template:

```yaml
card:
  template: |
    {{title}}
    {{#if priority}}[{{priority}}]{{/if}}
    {{assigneeName}}
  badges:
    - sla_status
    - issue_type
```

The built-in `sla_status` badge is green/amber/red; `issue_type` shows the configured icon.

## Filters

Restrict which issues show on a board:

```yaml
filters:
  labels: [customer-facing]
  issueTypes: [bug, feature]
  assignee: "@me"
```

Filters are YAML-declared so they live with the board's config, not hidden in each user's browser.

## Fallback columns

If you haven't written a board YAML yet, Kiket auto-generates one from your workflow's states. That's fine for demos; you'll want a real config to use swimlanes, WIP limits, or filters.

## Editing surfaces

The visual board editor lets you drag columns, configure swimlanes, and wire filters without touching YAML. Every save commits to your repo.
